attributes.description: Alerts when the request rate exceeds 1000 requests/minute on a host. High rates can be legitimate traffic, but should be corroborated against capacity and security signals.
attributes.name: [Apache OTel] High request rate

investigation_guide.blob:
## Apache high request rate

### What fired
Computed request rate (delta of `apache.requests` divided by elapsed seconds) exceeded 1000 requests/minute.

### Why it matters
A sudden traffic increase can legitimately precede a marketing event or cause a DoS-style overload. Either way, the server's remaining capacity headroom must be verified against worker pool utilisation and load averages.

### Triage
1. Overview dashboard: correlate request rate with busy-worker ratio and load averages.
2. Inspect `apache.traffic` rate — abnormal request-to-byte ratio may indicate an attack.
3. Look for spikes in error-adjacent signals (async connection accumulation, `no-requests-received` preceding the spike, etc.).
4. Use upstream CDN/WAF logs if available to identify source IPs / patterns.

### Remediation
- If legitimate, scale horizontally or raise `MaxRequestWorkers`.
- If attack, engage WAF / rate-limit at the edge.

### Tuning
- 1000 req/min is a reasonable small-to-mid tier threshold. Adjust to your baseline — compare against the 95th-percentile traffic over the past 7 days.

esqlQuery.esql:
// Alert triggers when the request rate exceeds the threshold.
// High request rate may indicate unexpected traffic spikes or potential DDoS attacks.
// Uses delta calculation for counter metric (requests is cumulative).
// The alert is grouped by host name.
// You can adjust the threshold value by modifying the request_rate in the WHERE clause (default: > 1000 requests/min).
FROM metrics-apachereceiver.otel-*
| STATS max_requests = MAX(metrics.apache.requests), min_requests = MIN(metrics.apache.requests),
        first_ts = MIN(@timestamp), last_ts = MAX(@timestamp)
  BY resource.attributes.host.name
| EVAL request_delta = max_requests - min_requests
| EVAL request_delta = CASE(request_delta >= 0, request_delta, 0)
| EVAL elapsed_sec = TO_DOUBLE(TO_LONG(last_ts) - TO_LONG(first_ts)) / 1000
| WHERE elapsed_sec >= 60
| EVAL request_rate = (request_delta / elapsed_sec) * 60
| WHERE request_rate > 1000
| EVAL request_rate = ROUND(request_rate, 2)
| KEEP resource.attributes.host.name, request_delta, request_rate, elapsed_sec
| SORT request_rate DESC
